title: Cienfuegosia flaviflora
taxonomy: Cienfuegosia flaviflora is a plant species in the Malvaceae family. It is native to Mexico and belongs to the genus Cienfuegosia. The plant is further classified into the order Malvales and the clade Rosidae within the angiosperm division of flowering plants. Cienfuegosia flaviflora is part of the class Magnoliopsida, which includes all flowering plants with a single cotyledon in the embryo. It is classified as a dicotyledonous plant, with a two-part seed leaf.

intro: Cienfuegosia flaviflora, also known as Yellow Cienfuegosia, is a plant species belonging to the Malvaceae family. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and infections. Cienfuegosia flaviflora contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
